data.title: PBOC is expected to set the USD/CNY reference rate at 7.2433 – Reuters estimate
data.description:
People's Bank of China USD/CNY reference rate is due around 0115 GMT.

The People's Bank of China (PBOC), China's central bank, is responsible for setting the daily midpoint of the yuan (also known as renminbi or RMB). The PBOC follows a managed floating exchange rate system that allows the value of the yuan to fluctuate within a certain range, called a "band," around a central reference rate, or "midpoint." It's currently at +/- 2%.

How the process works:

  • Daily midpoint setting: Each morning, the PBOC sets a midpoint for the yuan against a basket of currencies, primarily the US dollar. The central bank takes into account factors such as market supply and demand, economic indicators, and international currency market fluctuations. The midpoint serves as a reference point for that day's trading.
  • The trading band: The PBOC allows the yuan to move within a specified range around the midpoint. The trading band is set at +/- 2%, meaning the yuan could appreciate or depreciate by a maximum of 2% from the midpoint during a single trading day. This range is subject to change by the PBOC based on economic conditions and policy objectives.
  • Intervention: If the yuan's value approaches the limit of the trading band or experiences excessive volatility, the PBOC may intervene in the foreign exchange market by buying or selling the yuan to stabilize its value. This helps maintain a controlled and gradual adjustment of the currency's value.

data.title: Mayne Pharma to be Acquired by Cosette in AU$672 Million Deal; Shares Soar 33%
data.description:
Mayne Pharma Group entered into a scheme implementation deed to be acquired by Cosette via a scheme of arrangement for AU$7.40 per share, valuing the firm at around AU$672 million, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm's board unanimously recommended that its shareholders vote in favor of the scheme in the absence of a superior proposal.

The deal is fully funded and is subject to limited customary conditions, including regulatory approvals, approval by Mayne Pharma's shareholders, as well as court approval. It is expected to be completed between May and June.

Rubric Capital, which owns Mayne Pharma's convertible notes issued Dec. 31, 2022, agreed to divest the notes after the deal to the US-based Vista Healthcare Partners-backed firm for a value equivalent to the amount payable to Rubric.

Mayne Pharma's two largest shareholders, Viburnum Funds and Bruce Mathieson, who together own a 14.1% stake, plan to vote in favor of the proposed deal.

The recommendation is subject to an independent expert concluding that the deal is in the best interests of Mayne Pharma shareholders.

The firm's shares soared past 33% in early trading on Friday.

data.title: CME hogs drop with pork belly prices; exchange corrects price
data.description:
Chicago Mercantile Exchange lean hog futures slid on Thursday, as a sharp setback in pork belly prices set a negative tone for the market, brokers said.

Exchange-owner CME Group said it made an error on the final settlement price for February hog futures that it posted on Wednesday.

Futures declined for a second consecutive session under pressure from weaker pork cutout prices and profit-taking, brokers said.

Live cattle and feeder cattle futures also weakened, as a sell-off on Wall Street fueled concerns about consumer demand for meat, they said.

"The pullback in outside markets and really the soft prices for boxed beef and fresh pork have opened up the idea that it's time to take a little bit more profits," said Matthew Wiegand, commodity broker at FuturesOne in Nebraska.

April lean hog futures (LHJ25) finished down 1.225 cents at 88.525 cents per pound, after hitting their lowest price since February 4 earlier in the session.

CME Group said it revised the final settlement price for February hogs to 89.49 cents per pound from 90.19 cents.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture quoted wholesale pork carcass cutout at $94.03 per hundredweight late on Thursday, down by $1.49 from Wednesday. The decline followed a drop of $3.95 the previous day.

Belly values tumbled by $7.46, after sinking by $16.75 per hundredweight on Wednesday.

For boxed beef, prices for choice cuts fell by $1.26 while select cuts sagged by $0.58, the USDA said.

Declines in equities often weigh on cattle futures due to worries that demand for high-priced beef suffers when the economy weakens.

"The outside market nervousness is picking up the selling," Wiegand said. "Hopefully as we get into spring, we'll start seeing demand pick up."

March feeder cattle futures (FCH25) fell 2.2 cents to end at 266.825 cents per pound, after touching a one-week low earlier in the day.

April live cattle futures (LCJ25) closed down 0.975 cent at 193.8 cents per pound. The market remained near a 2025 low reached on Tuesday.

data.title: Nikkei Slips 0.1%, Dragged by Brokerage, Machinery Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks are lower in early trade as concerns about borrowing costs and U.S. tariffs continue. Brokerages and heavy-machinery makers are leading the declines. Nomura Holdings is down 1.0% and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries is 1.3% lower. The newest 10-year Japanese government bond yield is up 1.5 basis points at 1.455%, the highest level since November 2009, after Japan's stronger-than-expected inflation data. USD/JPY is at 149.70, down from 150.14 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock-market close. Investors are focusing on any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 0.1% at 38625.96. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Gold Edges Higher on Safe-Haven Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Gold edges higher in early Asian trade. The ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine adds a layer of complexity, says Inki Cho, Exness financial markets strategist consultant, noting that the U.S. began negotiations with Russia, raising hopes for a resolution. Steady progress in negotiations could strengthen risk appetite and apply pressure on the precious metal, Cho says in a note. Any setbacks could also reinforce gold's appeal as a safe-haven asset, Cho adds. Spot gold is up 0.1% at $2941.26/oz.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Air New Zealand's Maintenance Issues Remain Major Headache — Market Talk
data.description: Air New Zealand's 1H result was better than expected by Forsyth Barr and was accompanied by a NZ$100 million share buyback, but still came with enough baggage to keep the bank at neutral for now. Air New Zealand signaled its engine maintenance challenges have worsened. It now expects to have up to 11 planes at any time grounded for maintenance in 2H, up from eight in 1H. "Air New Zealand is at least two years away from a normalized engine maintenance schedule, which leaves investors in limbo as to what is an appropriate and sustainable (as is possible for an airline) level of profitability to use for relative valuation means," analyst Andy Bowley says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: ROSEN, GLOBAL INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages ESSA Pharma Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - EPIX
data.description:
NEW YORK, Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —

WHY: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of securities of ESSA Pharma Inc. between December 12, 2023 and October 31, 2024, inclusive (the “Class Period”), of the important March 25, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

SO WHAT: If you purchased ESSA Pharma Inc. securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the ESSA Pharma Inc. class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=34133 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than March 25, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs’ Bar. Many of the firm’s attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, defendants, throughout the Class Period, failed to disclose to investors that: (1) masofaniten in combination with enzalutamide had no clear efficacy benefit over enzalutamide alone; (2) accordingly, masofaniten in combination with enzalutamide was less effective in treating prostate cancer than defendants had led investors to believe; (3) the M-E Combination Study was unlikely to meet its prespecified Phase 2 primary endpoint; (4) accordingly, defendants had overstated masofaniten’s clinical, regulatory, and commercial prospects; and (5) as a result, defendants’ public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the ESSA Pharma Inc. class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=34133 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor’s ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

-------------------------------

data.title: Japan CPI Data Should Add to Case for More BOJ Monetary Tightening — Market Talk
data.description: Acceleration in Japan's headline inflation and strength in underlying inflation in January should add to the Bank of Japan's confidence that it can maintain its monetary tightening in coming quarters, Capital Economics' Toh Au Yu says in a commentary. Inflation excluding fresh food and energy edged upward from 2.4% in December to 2.5% in January, staying well above the central bank's forecast of 2.1% for FY 2025, the assistant economist notes. Recent acceleration in underlying inflation has mostly been driven by a surge in rice inflation, the economist adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's Mayne Pharma soars on buyout offer from Cosette Pharmaceuticals
data.description:
** Shares of Mayne Pharma rise 33.3% to A$7.25, their highest level since early-May 2024

** Stock set for biggest one-day pct gain since late-June 2016

** Co has agreed to sell all its shares to U.S.-based Cosette Pharmaceuticals through a scheme implementation deed

** Offer price at A$7.40 per share, a 37% premium to February 20 closing price

** 3.8 million shares change hands, highest since mid-December

** Stock is up 45.3% YTD

data.title: Perpetual Credit Income Trust Posts Lower Fiscal H1 Earnings, Investment Income
data.description:
Perpetual Credit Income Trust reported Thursday that its earnings per diluted unit in the fiscal first half fell to AU$0.0409, from AU$0.0531 per diluted unit a year earlier.

Total investment income for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$21.9 million, down 6% from AU$23.4 million in the same period last year.

The board declared a distribution of AU$0.006862 per unit, up from AU$0.006824 in the previous corresponding period, paid on Feb. 10.

data.title: UK Consumer Confidence Improves in February
data.description:
The GfK Consumer Confidence Index in the United Kingdom rose by 2 points to -20 in February 2025, reflecting a slight improvement as households expressed more optimism about their personal finances and the broader economic outlook.

Neil Bellamy, the consumer insights director at NIQ GfK, highlighted the shift from January, when all five key measures of consumer confidence declined, to February, when all of them saw an uptick.

Consumers also benefited from lower borrowing costs, offering some relief amid ongoing economic uncertainties, which have been intensified by US President Donald Trump’s tariff threats.

However, concerns remain about sluggish economic growth and persistent inflation, which could potentially lead to stagflation.

Recent data revealed that the UK economy grew at a near standstill in the second half of 2024, while inflation surged unexpectedly to a ten-month high of 3% in January.

data.title: Peter Warren Automotive Still Navigating Tricky Road — Market Talk
data.description: Whilst Peter Warren Automotive's 1H pretax profit was within its guidance range, it was a low bar to reach after the Australian car dealer downgraded expectations in December, Jefferies says. Peter Warren Automotive reported an underlying pretax profit of A$7.1 million, down 79% on a year ago. Its outlook statement was also downbeat, citing challenging conditions for new car sales. "It seems like more of the same for Autos--oversupply, soft demand and margin pressure with management focusing on hard to reduce operating expenses/revenue," analyst John Campbell says. "It's hard for us to see any material profit turnaround whilst these conditions persist." Jefferies has a hold call on Peter Warren Automotive, which is down 11% today. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1917 ET - Whilst Peter Warren Automotive's 1H pretax profit was within its guidance range, it was a low bar to reach after the Australian car dealer downgraded expectations in December, Jefferies says. Peter Warren Automotive reported an underlying pretax profit of A$7.1 million, down 79% on a year ago. Its outlook statement was also downbeat, citing challenging conditions for new car sales. "It seems like more of the same for Autos--oversupply, soft demand and margin pressure with management focusing on hard to reduce operating expenses/revenue," analyst John Campbell says. "It's hard for us to see any material profit turnaround whilst these conditions persist." Jefferies has a hold call on Peter Warren Automotive, which is down 11% today. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1856 ET - Block expects its 1Q guidance to be at the low point for both gross profit growth and adjusted operating income throughout the year, CFO Amrita Ahuja says on a call with analysts. The parent company of Cash App and Square is facing tougher foreign-exchange comparisons in the early part of 2025. One of the main objectives for the year is to grow Cash App Borrow, Ahuja says, noting that it plans to expand it to additional customers and offer higher limits by improving underwriting, expanding to more states and integrating borrow with direct deposit. Another focus is the recent launch of Afterpay on Cash App Card. "We see this product as another way for customers to manage their money and to drive increased spending through Cash App Card," Ahuja says. Shares fall 6.4%, to $77.70, after-hours. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1848 ET - Japanese stocks may fall as concerns about borrowing costs and U.S. tariffs continue. Nikkei futures are down 0.5% at 38530 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 149.42, down from 150.14 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Japan's consumer price index for January, excluding fresh food, rose 3.2% on year, compared with a 3.1% rise expected. Investors are focusing on any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.2% to 38678.04 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1830 ET - Live Nation Entertainment's plan to add 20 large venues, two of which will be located in Bogotá and Toronto, through 2026 should help the company add revenue growth. While the world's largest concert promoter is currently making money attractive returns from stadiums shows, it's not counting the beer money and the parking money it would get with its own venues. That would be a key component for Live Nation's financials given the high margins that come from the sale of beer and parking tickets. Live Nation shares fall 1.4% to $150.17 after hours. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1811 ET - Live Nation Entertainment is seeing consumers buy more tickets for stadium shows than ever before, CEO Michael Rapino says on a call with analysts. The world's largest concert promoter is selling over 75% of stadium tickets one week after putting them up for sale, at a much higher rate than a year earlier. There isn't a slowdown in growth at all, the executive says. "[We have] lots of lots of inventory, but equally great demand selling most of these stadiums out or are close to being sold out by the time we get to the show dates," Rapino says. Overall, Live Nation is growing its business through higher stadium volume. Shares trade 1.7% lower at $149.70 in post-market trading. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

1757 ET - Texas Roadhouse increased its commodity inflation expectations for 2025 to a 3% to 4% increase from 2024 levels, up from a 2% to 3% previously expected, largely due to rising beef prices. The casual-dining chain said in a 4Q earnings call that it hasn't seen herders retaining enough cattle for breeding, fueling its expectations that its main commodity will keep increasing in price. The chain posted a 7.7% 4Q same-store sales increase, and chain's shares rose 1.3% in after-market trading. (heather.haddon@wsj.com; @heatherhaddon)

1653 ET - Dining out at Texas Roadhouse is about to get more expensive. The restaurant chain says it plans to raise its menu prices by about 1.4% in early April. The increase comes as the company posts same-store sales up 7.7% at company restaurants in 4Q. Profit and revenue were higher in the recent quarter, and both came in above the expectations of analysts polled by FactSet. "These results are a credit to our operators who, as I've said before, continue to create an environment where Roadies want to work, and guests want to dine," CEO Jerry Morgan says.(connor.hart@wsj.com)

1632 ET - Australia's S&P/ASX 200 looks set to edge higher at the start of the week's final trading session, paring its hefty recent losses. ASX futures are up by 0.2% ahead of Friday's session, hinting at a modest rise for the benchmark index. The ASX 200 is 2.7% lower so far this week after four consecutive losses of growing size, culminating in Thursday's 1.1% slump. Ahead of the open, insurer QBE raised its dividend after its annual profit rose 27% on higher premiums and lower catastrophe costs. Gold miners Ramelius and Gold Road both reported increases profits. U.S. stocks provided a weak lead. The DJIA fell 1.0%, the S&P 500 slipped 0.4% and the Nasdaq Composite shed 0.5%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

1618 ET - The march toward $3,000 continues for gold, with the front-month contract settling up 0.7% to a record $2,940 per troy ounce. Global economic uncertainty continues to provide upside support for gold. While the inflation picture has improved worldwide, it remains an issue, and President Trump's tariff threats add volatility, although analysts hope they are still only a leverage tool. "We await further gyrations in the Administration's tariff policies, but still believe that, at least in part, Trump will use tariffs as a negotiating tool to motivate countries to cooperate with his key priorities," Citi says in a note. SPDR Gold gains 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1607 ET - U.S. stocks end broadly lower as weaker-than-expected sales guidance for the coming year from Walmart dragged down rivals. Walmart shares fall 6.5%, the worst among DJIA components. Financials were the weakest S&P 500 sector, with Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan losing 4.5%. Initial jobless claims rise more than expected, while manufacturing activity in the Philadelphia area expanded at a much slower pace in February, paring back some of the acceleration seen at the start of the year. DJIA falls 450 points, or 1%, to 44176, the S&P 500 loses 0.4% to 6117 and the Nasdaq slips 0.5% to 19962. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1539 ET - Clorox CEO Linda Rendle says that inflation-weary consumers are still willing to splurge on premium products, so long as they feel they are receiving better value. The company is seeing this trend play out with its trash bags, she says at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York conference. Clorox earlier this year launched a product improvement to its most premium trash bag line, strengthening the top of the bag where rips are most likely to occur. "We're seeing very significant growth from this claim and innovation, with 14% sales growth in our MaxStrength line," Rendle says. The company will test the trend again with the launch of Fresh Step Heavy Duty, a cat litter with a 30-day odor-control claim, which Rendle says is the longest on the market. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1532 ET - Walmart's grocery category remains a "standout category with mid-single digit growth," CEO Douglas McMillon says on a call with analysts, even if it hasn't been able to surpass that category growth rate in the fiscal year. That shows what a "standout" performance is for the retail giant in the current environment. Walmart remains committed to lower prices in the midst of food inflation as merchandise general sales remain a headwind. Walmart doesn't expect its general merchandise category to strengthen anytime soon. Target's 4Q results, expected on March 11, will likely reflect that weakness. Target shares hit a 52-week low in November, after it posted disappointing 3Q results and lowered its profit and sales goals for the year. Walmart and Target trade 6.3% and 2.1% lower, respectively. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: Silex Systems Posts Fiscal H1 Loss of AU$0.076 Per Share, Revenue of AU$6.7 Million
IAMGOLD Corp reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• IAMGOLD Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 10 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 6 cents. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 13 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 7 cents to 16 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 57.9% to $469.90 million from a year ago; analysts expected $489.70 million.

  • IAMGOLD Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was 15 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $86.2 million.

  • IAMGOLD Corp shares had risen by 19.1% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 22.6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, three analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the gold peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for IAMGOLD Corp is C$10.75

data.title: Ouhua Energy Expects Fiscal Year 2024 Loss as Gas Demand Slows
data.description:
Ouhua Energy Holdings expects to report a net loss for 2024, citing weaker sales amid a slump in liquefied natural gas prices and slowing industrial demand in China, according to a Thursday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The company said lower gas prices had dampened market demand for liquefied petroleum gas, while China's economic slowdown weighed on industrial consumption.

data.title: Lenovo Says Winston Cheng Appointed Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
Lenovo Group Ltd :

  • WINSTON, SHAO-MIN CHENG HAS BEEN APPOINTED TO ASSUME POSITION OF CFO

  • RETIREMENT OF WONG WAI MING AS CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER

  • LAURA GREEN QUATELA RETIRE AS SENIOR VICE PRESIDENT

  • WONG WAI MING WILL RETIRE AS AN EXECUTIVE VICE PRESIDENT

data.title: JGBs Edge Lower as Hotter-Than-Expected Japan CPI Weighs — Market Talk
data.description: JGBs edge lower in price terms in the morning Tokyo session, weighed by hotter-than-expected Japan CPI which may add to the case for further BOJ rate increases. Core consumer prices rose 3.2% on year in January, above economists' expectations for a 3.1% increase. Recent weakness in the domestic government bond market is likely to persist against the backdrop of revised perceptions for the BOJ's rate-hike trajectory, Mitsubishi UFJ Morgan Stanley Securities fixed income strategists say. However, declines in U.S. Treasury yields and yen appreciation may provide some support, the strategists say in a research report. The 10-year JGB yield is up 1bp at 1.450%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Ecolab Declares Dividend of 65C >ECL
Clearbridge Health Allocates S$485,000 from Share Placement to Debt Payments
data.description:
Clearbridge Health has utilized SG$485,000 from the SG$929,450 net proceeds raised through a share placement for loan principal and interest payments, according to a Thursday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The remaining SG$444,000 will be used for general working capital.

data.title: Globus Medical Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary

The mean expectation of sixteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 75 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 65 cents to 88 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 6.6% to $657.29 million from a year ago; analysts expected $643.38 million.

  • Globus Medical Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 19 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $26.51 million.

  • Globus Medical Inc shares had risen by 1.7% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The average consensus recommendation for the medical equipment, supplies & distribution peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Globus Medical Inc is $100.00

data.title: Accrelist's Jubilee Unit Secures Shareholder Approval for Plastic Business Sale
data.description:
Accrelist Ltd. said shareholders of its subsidiary, Jubilee Industries Holdings, have approved the proposed sale of the plastic business of We Total Engineering, according to a Thursday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

The resolutions were passed at an extraordinary general meeting held on Feb. 20.

data.title: Australia's QBE Insurance hits near 15-year high on strong FY results, forecast
data.description:
** Shares of QBE Insurance rise as much as 8% to A$21.670, their highest since late-April, 2010

** Co says FY adjusted NPAT rose 27% to $1.73 billion, beating consensus estimate of $1.67 billion

** Declares final dividend of 63 Australian cents per share, higher than consensus estimate of 57 cents

** Expects its gross written premiums to grow in mid-single digits for fiscal 2025, better than consensus estimate of 2.8% growth

** Stock among top five gainers on ASX 200 benchmark index

** Stock up ~12% YTD, including day's moves

data.title: Sunrise Shares Raises SG$5 Million Through Unsecured Loans
data.description:
Sunrise Shares Holdings has secured SG$5 million in unsecured loans from three lenders, including its chief executive officer, to fund working capital and potential investments, according to a Thursday filing on the Singapore Exchange.

CEO Huang Jyun-Ruei provided an interest-free loan of SG$1.8 million, while Cybersec Technology and investor Xue Jie each extended loans of SG$1.8 million and SG$1.5 million, respectively, at an annual interest rate of 6%.

The company said the funding allows it to deploy capital more efficiently and at a lower cost compared with other financing options.

The loans, which mature in 12 months, can be repaid in full or in tranches.

data.title: JPMorgan had 'buyer's remorse,' lawyer says as startup founder's trial starts
data.description:
The criminal trial of Charlie Javice for allegedly defrauding JPMorgan Chase into buying her college financial aid startup Frank began on Thursday, with her lawyer arguing there was no fraud and the banking giant merely had "buyer's remorse."

Javice, 32, has pleaded not guilty to securities fraud, wire fraud, bank fraud and conspiracy over JPMorgan's $175 million purchase of Frank in July 2021.

Prosecutors accused Javice of lying by assuring the largest U.S. bank that Frank had 4.25 million customers when it had only about 300,000.

JPMorgan allegedly discovered the inflated number when it tried to contact customers it believed were real to sell products, and received far fewer responses than expected.

Jose Baez, a lawyer for Javice, told jurors during his opening argument in Manhattan federal court that JPMorgan undertook extensive due diligence into Frank, and knew how many clients it had before completing the purchase.

He said JPMorgan claimed it was hoodwinked only a year later when financial aid regulations changed, and fraud was the only condition that allowed it to back out from the sale contract.

Earlier, prosecutor Rushmi Bhaskaran said in her opening argument that Javice collected fake names and addresses from a college friend because JPMorgan required the data.

Bhaskaran also said Javice's co-defendant Olivier Amar, who was Frank's chief growth officer, bought "sham lists" of student data from third parties for Frank to falsely pass off as customers.

"It was through their lies that they became multimillionaires," Bhaskaran said.

Jonathan Cogan, a lawyer for Amar, told jurors his client was "out of the loop" during negotiations with JPMorgan.

Javice, a Florida resident, studied at the University of Pennsylvania's Wharton School, and appeared on Forbes magazine's "30 Under 30" list in 2019.

The trial is before U.S. District Judge Alvin Hellerstein, who seated jurors and alternates earlier on Thursday.

JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has called the Frank acquisition a "huge mistake."

The bank declined on Thursday to comment on the trial.

Founded in 2017, Frank was marketed as a tool to help simplify college financial aid for students and parents.

Javice had discussed a transaction with another bank, Capital One , before turning to JPMorgan, Amar's lawyer said.

data.title: Nexa Resources SA reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Nexa Resources SA reported a quarterly adjusted loss of 100 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -71 cents. The mean expectation of six analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 27 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 6 cents to 43 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 17.6% to $741.00 million from a year ago; analysts expected $707.14 million.

  • Nexa Resources SA's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 74 cents​.

  • Nexa Resources SA shares had fallen by 39.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 35.4% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the specialty mining & metals peer group is also "hold"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Nexa Resources SA is $7.80
